subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Build Shape City by naming, describing, composing, and partitioning shapes.
lesson.objective: I can shapes and spatial reasoning.
lesson.concept_explanation: Shapes and Spatial Reasoning means using a math model, saying what you see, and checking that the answer fits.
lesson.common_misconception: Use attributes, not just looks, to name a shape.
lesson.real_world_connection: Build Shape City by naming, describing, composing, and partitioning shapes.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model and careful counting to solve shapes and spatial reasoning problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Use shape identification to reason carefully before choosing or writing an answer.

worked_examples.text: Watch the model first, then match it to the answer for shapes and spatial reasoning.
Demo: count, compare, or match each part before answering.

hint_ladder: Say the math words out loud.
Check your answer by doing the action again.

hints: Look at the model carefully.
Count or compare one step at a time.
Check that your answer matches the question.
guided_practice.explanation: The correct answer is circle because the model matches the prompt.
guided_practice.feedback: Nice work: circle matches the model.

misconception_bank.shape_label_confusion: Use attributes, not just looks, to name a shape.
misconception_bank.corner_side_confusion: Sides are straight edges; corners are where sides meet.
misconception_bank.rotation_confusion: A shape keeps its name when turned.
misconception_bank.defining_attribute_confusion: Defining attributes decide the shape name.
misconception_bank.partition_confusion: Equal parts should be the same size.

story.text: Welcome to Shape City Quest. Shape City needs builders who can name, describe, compose, and partition shapes. Your goal is to use shape attributes to decide what a shape is. You will learn to look for sides, corners, curves, and equal parts instead of guessing by looks alone. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about shapes and spatial reasoning. Important words are shape, side, corner, attribute, compose, and partition. The shape identification model shows a shape so you can describe what you notice, such as curved sides or three corners. A common mistake is naming a shape by how it looks at first glance, so use attributes to prove the name. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The prompt asks what shape is shown. Step one, look at the model and name its attributes. Step two, check whether it has curves, sides, and corners. Step three, match those attributes to a shape name. The final answer can be circle when the model is round with no corners. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how to identify a shape by its attributes. Watch for sides, corners, and curves before choosing a name. For example, a triangle has 3 corners and 3 sides, while a circle is round and has no corners. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the attribute strategy: look for sides, corners, curves, and equal parts. The model shows a shape to identify or describe, such as a circle or triangle. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It may ask about corners, parts of a shape, or a shape turned a different way. Use attributes, not just the way the shape is facing. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you can name and describe shapes using their attributes. Count sides or corners carefully and check that the shape name matches the model. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work building Shape City. You practiced naming shapes, describing attributes, and checking corners, sides, and curves. Shape attributes help you explain your thinking like a mathematician. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your shape practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep building geometry skills. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
